briefing.opening: Good morning. We will now begin the climate briefing for May 4, 2026. Today, various efforts and discussions to address the intensifying global climate crisis have been actively reported, alongside warnings about its severity.

sections.title: 🔴 Unprecedented Acceleration of the Climate Crisis and Its Direct Impacts
sections.content: Recently published reports and expert warnings present a grim outlook, stating that this year is projected to be the hottest on record, with temperatures 1.5 degrees Celsius above average this spring and unprecedented heatwaves expected this summer [1, 8]. This climate change is causing heat-related illnesses and economic damage due to a month-long heatwave in India, and research has also shown that heatwaves increase the risk of stroke and death [12, 84]. Domestically, marine heatwaves have severely impacted the catch of popular fish like tuna and rockfish, while in tropical Africa, a massive drought is occurring and Magellanic penguins are being affected by climate change, making severe threats visible across entire ecosystems [2, 13, 82]. Furthermore, precise observations have confirmed that atmospheric greenhouse gas concentrations have reached 'all-time high' levels, further escalating concerns about the deepening climate crisis [3, 5].

sections.title: ⚠️ Emerging Threats: The Severity of Microplastics and Methane Emissions
sections.content: Recent studies warn that microplastics are an emerging threat accelerating global warming. Specifically, it has been suggested that heat-trapping microplastics play a direct role in climate change, and that atmospheric microplastics could exacerbate global warming [6, 15, 35, 44, 55, 79]. Meanwhile, the issue of methane emissions is also being seriously addressed. Satellite observations have revealed 'super-emitter' sources of methane, with some pointing out that this represents a system failure beyond mere technical problems [11, 14]. The International Energy Agency (IEA) emphasized that addressing methane emissions is crucial for both climate change and energy security, analyzing that energy sector methane emissions remained at record high levels in 2025 [36, 95]. Large landfills worldwide are also facing the threat of methane, making urgent and proactive responses to these two pollutants imperative [86].

sections.title: 🌱 Domestic and International Efforts and Technological Progress for Carbon Neutrality
sections.content: Various domestic and international efforts and technological advancements to address the climate crisis are also actively underway. South Chungcheong Province is pursuing carbon neutrality in the building sector through green building policies, while Korea East-West Power, Korea Midland Power, and Korea Southern Power are actively engaged in renewable energy and greenhouse gas reduction activities [18, 19, 26]. The government is working to remove barriers to renewable energy permits, and local efforts to expand renewable energy are continuing, with Muan-gun and Hongcheon-gun recruiting participants for integrated renewable energy support projects [21, 24, 27, 28, 31]. Internationally, China has launched a project to directly connect renewable energy to data centers, and New York officials are encouraging everyday carbon reduction efforts, such as promoting composting in response to climate change [20, 46]. Furthermore, new research has shown that renewable energy is more cost-effective than direct air capture for carbon reduction, once again underscoring the importance of transitioning to renewable energy [93].

sections.title: 🌍 Climate Policy, Legal Disputes, and the Challenge of Adaptation
sections.content: Policy responses to climate change and legal disputes over responsibility are also emerging as major issues. In the United States, lawsuits related to the Trump administration have been reported by various media outlets, including the federal government suing the state of Minnesota to halt climate change-related legal actions taken by Minnesota against the oil industry [34, 42, 48, 50, 58, 64, 67, 68, 70, 71, 72, 73, 74, 76]. This is considered an example demonstrating the 'overall' uncertainty of climate change litigation [60]. Meanwhile, reports indicate that the government's strategy to protect critical infrastructure from climate change is inadequate, and criticisms have also arisen regarding the limited usefulness of flood map portals that do not reflect climate change [75, 77, 80, 83, 88, 89, 90, 91]. In this context, the urgency of climate change adaptation is being emphasized, with research indicating that New Orleans has reached an 'irreversible point' due to sea-level rise and must begin immediate relocation [94].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlighted both the severity of the climate crisis and the international community's diverse efforts and policy challenges to address it. Moving forward, we must continue to strive for practical solutions and responses based on a scientific understanding of climate change. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.title: Hyundai Department Store's 'Vinyl Recycling' Shines Brighter Amid High Oil Prices
articles.original_title: 고유가에 더 빛났다, 현대백화점 ‘비닐 재생’
articles.description: 200,000 bags secured through 'self-procurement'. As the price of naphtha, a raw material for vinyl, surged due to the impact of the Middle East war, Hyundai Department Store's waste vinyl resource circulation process, 'Vinyl to Vinyl,' is playing a crucial role. The vinyl recycling activities, part of an ongoing eco-friendly campaign, are establishing themselves as a self-sufficient model for procuring resources during emergencies. Hyundai Department Store has secured 200,000 plastic bags through 'Vinyl to Vinyl' for department store operations...

summary.phenomenon: Hyundai Department Store has secured 200,000 plastic bags through its waste vinyl resource circulation process, 'Vinyl to Vinyl.' This initiative is functioning as a self-sufficient resource model amidst the surge in naphtha prices caused by the Middle East war.
summary.cause: The price of naphtha, a raw material for vinyl, surged due to the impact of the Middle East war. Consequently, Hyundai Department Store's vinyl recycling activities, which were part of an eco-friendly campaign, have taken on the role of emergency resource procurement.

articles.original_description: 봉투 20만장 확보 ‘자체 조달’중동전쟁 영향으로 비닐의 원료가 되는 나프타 가격이 급등하자 현대백화점의 폐비닐 자원순환 프로세스 ‘비닐 투 비닐’이 제 역할을 톡톡히 하고 있다. 친환경 캠페인 일환으로 이어온 비닐 재생산 활동이 비상시 자원을 스스로 조달하는 자급형 모델로 자리 잡고 있다.현대백화점은 비닐 투 비닐을 통해 비닐봉투 20만장을 확보, 백화점···

articles.title: Kia Overtakes Hyundai for the First Time... Domestic Sales Up 7.9% Amid High Oil Prices
articles.original_title: 기아, 현대차 첫 추월…고유가에 국내 판매량 7.9% ↑
articles.description: Sorento HEV, ranked #1, drives upward trend. Strengths in EV and RV lead to 'escape' from perpetual second place. For the first time since its incorporation into Hyundai Motor Group in 1998, Kia surpassed Hyundai's domestic car sales last month. Kia, long considered the second-place contender, has now overtaken Hyundai, which has consistently held the top spot in monthly domestic sales. Amid the era of high oil prices driven by factors such as the Middle East war, sales of Kia's strong vehicle types, including hybrid electric vehicles (HEV), electric vehicles (EV), and recreational vehicles (RV), have seen an upward trend...

summary.phenomenon: Last month, Kia surpassed Hyundai's monthly sales in the domestic automotive market for the first time since its incorporation into Hyundai Motor Group in 1998. Kia's domestic sales increased by 7.9%.
summary.cause: The advent of an era of high oil prices, influenced by factors such as the Middle East war, drove an increase in sales for Kia's strong vehicle segments, including hybrid electric vehicles (HEV), electric vehicles (EV), and recreational vehicles (RV).

articles.original_description: ‘1위’ 쏘렌토 HEV 등 상승세 견인EV·RV 강점…만년 2위 ‘탈출’기아가 1998년 현대자동차그룹에 편입된 이후 처음으로 지난달 국내 자동차 시장에서 현대차 판매 대수를 앞질렀다. 만년 2위였던 기아가 월간 국내 판매량에서 선두를 지켜온 현대차를 앞선 것이다. 중동전쟁 등으로 인한 고유가 시대를 맞아 하이브리드차(HEV)와 전기차(EV), 레저용 차량···

articles.original_title: ‘Point of no return’: New Orleans relocation must start now due to sea level, study finds
articles.description: Louisiana’s cultural hotspot could be surrounded by Gulf of Mexico before end of this century, authors sayThe process of relocating people from New Orleans should start immediately, as the city has reached a “point of no return” that will see it surrounded by the ocean within decades due to the climate crisis, a stark new study has concluded.Ongoing sea-level rise and the rampant erosion of wetlands in southern Louisiana will swallow up the New Orleans area within a few generations, with the new

summary.phenomenon: According to a new study, New Orleans, Louisiana, USA, has reached a 'point of no return' due to the climate crisis and could be surrounded by the Gulf of Mexico within decades, specifically before the end of this century.
summary.cause: This is due to ongoing sea-level rise and the rapid erosion of wetlands in southern Louisiana.
summary.outlook: The study suggests that relocation procedures for residents should begin immediately, as the New Orleans area is expected to be submerged by the sea within a few generations.

articles.title: Fixing methane mega-leaks could boost energy stock amid crisis, report says
articles.description: International Energy Agency analysis shows methane leaks remained at near-record highs in 2025Methane emissions from the energy sector remained at near record levels in 2025, the International Energy Agency has concluded.Tackling the emissions could make billions of cubic metres of gas available to international markets, a top priority as the war in the Middle East squeezes energy supplies, the IEA said in a report. Continue reading...

summary.outlook: The IEA report states that tackling these methane emissions could make billions of cubic meters of gas available to international markets, which is a top priority amidst energy supply shortages caused by the Middle East conflict.
